title: The Investor-Ready Marketplace Financial Model
subtitle: Stop Mixing Up GMV and Net Revenue. Start Raising Like a Pro.
descriptionBody: If you are building a two-sided marketplace, you already have the hardest job in the startup world: solving the chicken-and-egg problem. You shouldn't also have to spend 40 hours building a custom spreadsheet to figure out if your unit economics actually make sense. Most templates out there treat marketplaces like standard SaaS companies, which is a guaranteed way to get laughed out of a VC pitch.
section1Body: This is a battle-tested, clean-code 3-year projection engine built specifically for horizontal two-sided marketplaces. It handles the heavy lifting of dual-sided acquisition, GMV waterfalls, and marketplace-specific valuation so you can stop wrestling with formulas and get back to matching supply and demand.

features.name: The Dual-Sided Growth Engine
features.desc: Buyers and Sellers are not the same. This model lets you project supply and demand entirely separately so you can prove your network effects scale.

features.name: The GMV to Revenue Waterfall
features.desc: Stop confusing GMV with net revenue. Input your AOV, take rate, and frequency; watch your GMV cleanly translate into the actual revenue you keep.

features.name: The Marketplace Benchmarks
features.desc: Automatically compare your Buyer-to-Seller Ratio, Take Rate, and Contribution Margin against the top-tier marketplaces investors already know.

features.name: The Base / Bull / Bear Scenarios
features.desc: Switch your entire model between Conservative, Base, and Optimistic cases with one click. Walk into your pitch with the right story ready.

features.name: The Triple-Method Valuation Engine
features.desc: Marketplaces are valued differently. Get your Pre-Money estimated using GMV Multiples, Net Revenue Multiples, and DCF — the three methods VCs actually use.

whyPoints.title: Marketplaces Are Not SaaS
whyPoints.desc: Trying to fit your marketplace into a SaaS template is a guaranteed way to confuse investors and misprice your round. This model speaks the right language.

whyPoints.title: Prove Your Network Effects
whyPoints.desc: VCs want to see that supply and demand grow together and reinforce each other. This model shows exactly that — in a clean, single spreadsheet.

whyPoints.title: Pitch-Ready from Day One
whyPoints.desc: Built-in scenario planner so you can walk into every investor meeting with Conservative, Base, and Optimistic cases ready to present.

footerResultDetails: Total clarity on your marketplace's financial future.
whatYouGet: A complete, unlocked 3-year financial projection spreadsheet (Excel format).
Dual-Sided Growth Models to independently forecast Supply (Sellers) and Demand (Buyers).
Automated GMV & Net Revenue waterfalls based on your specific take rate.
The "Vibe Check" Dashboard summarizing your GMV, Contribution Margin, and Runway.
A Triple-Method Valuation Engine (GMV Multiples, Net Rev Multiples, and DCF).
Step-by-step "Start Here" guide to populate the model in under 30 minutes.
whoThisIsFor: Two-sided marketplace founders (B2B or B2C) raising a seed or Series A round.
Startups struggling to balance buyer CAC against seller acquisition costs (SAC).
Founders who need to prove their network effects and unit economics actually scale.

faq.q: Does this work for both B2B and B2C marketplaces?
faq.a: Yes! Whether you are matching freelancers with enterprises (B2B) or individuals selling used sneakers (B2C), the core dual-sided mechanics (supply vs. demand, GMV, take rate) remain exactly the same.

faq.q: I'm not a finance person. Is this too complex?
faq.a: Not at all. I survived the trenches of CA prep so you don't have to. Just follow the step-by-step "Start Here" guide and plug your numbers into the blue cells. The model automatically does the heavy lifting.

faq.q: Can I run different growth scenarios for my pitch deck?
faq.a: Absolutely. There is a built-in Scenario Selector tab. You can plug in your conservative, base, and optimistic assumptions, and flip the entire model between them with a single drop-down menu.
